old_value: {"id":"AdiabaticGrover-1ba","title":"Define IsStoquastic: WHT of b is non-positive off the DC component","description":"IsStoquastic n b := ∀ k : BoolVec n, k ≠ 0 → (∑_z b z * (-1)^bitDot k z) ≤ 0. This is the condition B̂(k) ≤ 0 for k ≠ 0.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"lennart.binkowski@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-05T13:51:10Z","created_by":"lennartbinkowski","updated_at":"2026-03-05T13:51:10Z"}

{"id":"AdiabaticGrover-9c6","title":"Prove Hb_entry: matrix entry formula for H_b via WHT of b","description":"(Hb n b) x y = (∑_z b(z) * (-1)^{bitDot (x⊕y) z}) / 2^n. Follows from unfolding W = Matrix.of whtEntry and matrix multiplication.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"lennart.binkowski@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-05T13:51:02Z","created_by":"lennartbinkowski","updated_at":"2026-03-05T13:51:02Z"}

old_value: {"id":"AdiabaticGrover-9j4","title":"Axiomatize Perron-Frobenius for irreducible non-positive off-diagonal matrices","description":"axiom perron_frobenius: real symmetric matrix with strictly negative off-diagonal entries has a simple minimum eigenvalue with a strictly positive eigenvector. Check Mathlib first (search for PerronFrobenius, Irreducible, Matrix.pos_eigenvalue); axiomatize if absent.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"lennart.binkowski@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-05T13:51:23Z","created_by":"lennartbinkowski","updated_at":"2026-03-05T14:02:51Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T14:02:51Z","close_reason":"Axiomatized Perron-Frobenius in PerronFrobenius.lean"}

new_value: Axiomatized Perron-Frobenius in PerronFrobenius.lean; searched Mathlib (not present in v4.28.0)

{"description":"Use Rellich + Hint_unique_ground_state_ns to build the analytic curve. Proof: (1) rellich_eigenvalues gives analytic branches σ₁,…,σ_N. (2) For t∈(0,1), Hint_unique_ground_state_ns gives unique minimum eigenvalue — so one branch is the minimum. (3) The branch at t=0 equals min_x b(x) (by Hb_eigenvalues). (4) That branch stays as unique min throughout (0,1) — no crossings possible since min is simple. (5) By continuity of the analytic branch, value at t=1 = lim_{t→1} λ_min(H(t)) = min_x c(x). Also needs: import AdiabaticGrover.PerronFrobenius in MainThm.lean."}
old_value: {"id":"AdiabaticGrover-lo8","title":"Prove stoquastic_implies_analytic_curve: IsStoquastic b → ∀ c, ∃ analytic curve from min b to min c","description":"Non-degenerate ground state for all t ∈ (0,1) + rellich_eigenvalues axiom gives a unique analytic minimum branch. Endpoints pin to min b at t=0 (Hb_eigenvalues) and min c at t=1 (Hc_eigenvalues). This fills in analytic_eigcurve_iff on the sufficient-condition side.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"lennart.binkowski@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-05T13:51:36Z","created_by":"lennartbinkowski","updated_at":"2026-03-05T13:51:36Z"}
